keyPoints: Feature extraction identifies and isolates relevant information from raw image data.
Common techniques include edge detection (e.g., Canny), corner detection (e.g., Harris), and blob detection (e.g., SIFT).
The goal is to reduce dimensionality and represent an image by its most distinctive and invariant features.
commonMistakes: Confusing feature extraction with feature detection; extraction describes the feature, detection finds it.
Using a technique that is not invariant to necessary transformations (e.g., scale, rotation).
Applying an edge detector without proper image smoothing, leading to noise being mistaken for features.
